hypotheses.title: LOX Crosslink Density as Bond Occupation Probability Predicts a Universal Immune Exclusion Threshold at p ~ 0.21-0.25
hypotheses.key_attack: Contact guidance in aligned fibers (Salmon 2012, Pruitt 2019) challenges the random lattice assumption; Saha 2024 shows continuously varying exponents for active particles, potentially invalidating the specific numerical predictions (nu=0.88, beta=0.417)
hypotheses.key_defense: Genuine novelty confirmed (zero prior work); all citations verified; ECM topology dominates over chemotaxis in dense tumors (literature confirmed); highly falsifiable with specific experimental protocol
hypotheses.nearest_kill_reason: Fiber alignment may place real ECM in anisotropic/directed percolation class rather than isotropic bond percolation, changing both p_c and exponents

critic_questions.question: How does the LOX-to-p mapping work quantitatively? If LOX crosslinks form preferentially at high-strain regions, the bond closure is correlated. How does this affect p_c?

hypotheses.title: Correlation Length Exponent nu = 0.88 Predicts T Cell Cluster Size Distribution Near Hot-Cold Boundary
hypotheses.key_attack: Non-uniqueness of power-law exponents (multiple mechanisms produce power laws); lattice constant ambiguity creates factor-of-5 uncertainty in quantitative predictions; competing explanations for T cell clustering (chemokines, antigen, vasculature, TLS)
hypotheses.key_defense: Mathematical framework is rigorous; parameter-free prediction (tau~2.19); spatial transcriptomics datasets exist for testing
hypotheses.nearest_kill_reason: Power-law cluster size distributions arise from branching processes, preferential attachment, and SOC, making tau~2.19 non-unique to percolation

critic_questions.question: What is the correct lattice constant? The factor-of-5 ambiguity (a=2 um vs a=10 um) shifts all quantitative predictions. Can you derive it from first principles?

hypotheses.title: Finite-Size Scaling Explains Discordant Infiltration Scores Between Biopsies and Resections
hypotheses.key_attack: Qualitative prediction (peak variance at intermediate scores) is trivially expected from any spatially heterogeneous process without percolation theory; clinical literature attributes biopsy discordance to genomic/immunological heterogeneity, not ECM topology; quantitative prediction (L^(-1/nu) scaling) requires impractical multi-gauge biopsy experiment
hypotheses.key_defense: Finite-size scaling is mathematically rigorous; biopsy discordance is a real clinical problem
hypotheses.nearest_kill_reason: Trivial prediction dressed in percolation language (killed on triviality + counter-evidence + low groundedness)

hypotheses.title: Collagen I/III Ratio Acts as Lattice Topology Switch Shifting p_c, Explaining Macrophage-Mediated Cold-to-Hot Conversion
hypotheses.key_attack: Collagen I and III differ in biochemistry (stiffness, integrin binding, fiber diameter) not just topology; macrophage depletion has many non-ECM effects confounding experimental isolation; specific p_c values (0.35-0.45) are entirely speculative
hypotheses.key_defense: Fusilier 2025 provides strong biological grounding; anisotropy increasing p_c confirmed in percolation literature; same exponents/shifted p_c is non-trivial prediction
hypotheses.nearest_kill_reason: Biochemical confounds between collagen types make it impossible to isolate topology-only effects in real experiments

critic_questions.question: Can you design an experiment that isolates the TOPOLOGY effect of collagen I/III switching from BIOCHEMISTRY effects (stiffness, integrin binding)?

hypotheses.title: LOX Inhibitor Dose-Response Follows Order Parameter Scaling P_inf ~ (p-p_c)^0.417, Predicting Therapeutic Window
hypotheses.key_attack: Multiple nonlinear transformations between drug dose and effective p (enzyme kinetics, time-dependent crosslink formation, non-reversal of existing crosslinks); BAPN non-specific (inhibits all LOX family); context-dependent LOX effects (tumor-promoting in some contexts)
hypotheses.key_defense: Therapeutic window prediction (dose near p_c, not MTD) is clinically novel and actionable; beta=0.417 is parameter-free; no existing dose-response titration data to contradict
hypotheses.nearest_kill_reason: BAPN does not break existing crosslinks, so dose-to-p mapping depends critically on ECM turnover timescale, which may be too slow for the percolation scaling to emerge

critic_questions.question: BAPN prevents new crosslinks but does NOT break existing ones. How long must LOX inhibition continue before effective p changes significantly? What is the ECM turnover timescale?

hypotheses.title: Active T Cell Self-Trapping Near Percolation Threshold Explains Paradoxical Margin Accumulation
hypotheses.key_attack: Three prerequisites for self-trapping fail: Pe~3 is below threshold (Pe>>10 needed), T cells are deformable (not rigid), T cells use amoeboid crawling with frequent direction changes (not persistent propulsion); well-established immunological explanations for margin accumulation
hypotheses.key_defense: Self-trapping phenomenon is real for synthetic active particles; genuinely novel application to immunology
hypotheses.nearest_kill_reason: Mechanism requires conditions (high Pe, rigid particles, persistent propulsion) that T cells do not meet (killed on mechanism failure)

hypotheses.title: Universality Class Exponents Are Tumor-Type-Invariant, Enabling Pan-Cancer Percolation Model
hypotheses.key_attack: Different tumor types have different degrees of fiber alignment; strongly anisotropic systems may fall in directed percolation universality class with different exponents; Saha 2024 shows continuously varying exponents for active particles; this is highest-risk/highest-reward hypothesis
hypotheses.key_defense: Universality is one of the most robust results in statistical physics; prediction is highly falsifiable and sharp; Harris criterion may protect against short-range correlations
hypotheses.nearest_kill_reason: Different tumor ECM anisotropies (PDAC strongly aligned vs lung loosely isotropic) may place them in different universality classes, destroying the pan-cancer claim

critic_questions.question: Given Saha 2024 continuously varying exponents for active particles, what specific active-particle-corrected exponents would you predict for T cells at Pe~3? Are these universal if Pe varies between tumors?

hypotheses.title: TGF-beta-LOX Feedback as Bond-Correlated Percolation Predicts Super-Additive LOX + Anti-TGF-beta Synergy
hypotheses.key_attack: TGF-beta blockade has many non-percolation immunological effects (Treg depletion, direct T cell activation, EMT reversal) making experimental isolation of de-correlation mechanism very difficult; TGF-beta signaling range is uncertain (10-100 um)
hypotheses.key_defense: Positive bond correlation raising p_c confirmed by independent percolation literature; all biological components (TGF-beta-LOX feedback, Kuczek 2019 collagen-TGF-beta) verified; specific experimental protocol with SHG imaging; no counter-evidence found
hypotheses.nearest_kill_reason: TGF-beta blockade has so many immunological effects that even if LOX + anti-TGF-beta synergy is observed, attributing it to percolation de-correlation is very difficult

critic_questions.question: If TGF-beta correlations decay exponentially with range ~50 um (25 lattice constants), how large is the predicted p_c shift? Is it 0.01 (negligible) or 0.1 (significant)?
How does the LOX-to-p mapping work quantitatively? If LOX crosslinks form preferentially at high-strain regions, the bond closure is correlated. How does this affect p_c?
What is the correct lattice constant? The factor-of-5 ambiguity (a=2 um vs a=10 um) shifts all quantitative predictions. Can you derive it from first principles?
Can you design an experiment that isolates the TOPOLOGY effect of collagen I/III switching from BIOCHEMISTRY effects (stiffness, integrin binding)?
BAPN prevents new crosslinks but does NOT break existing ones. How long must LOX inhibition continue before effective p changes significantly? What is the ECM turnover timescale?
Given Saha 2024 continuously varying exponents for active particles, what active-particle-corrected exponents would you predict for T cells at Pe~3? Are these universal if Pe varies between tumors?
If TGF-beta correlations decay exponentially with range ~50 um (25 lattice constants), how large is the predicted p_c shift? Is it 0.01 (negligible) or 0.1 (significant)?

meta_critique.kill_rate_assessment: At 25%, slightly below the 30-50% target range. However, 4 of 6 survivors are WOUNDED with significant downgrades. The two kills (H3, H6) are well-justified: H3 on triviality (peak variance at intermediate values is trivially expected), H6 on mechanism failure (three prerequisites fail for T cells). The two survivors (H1, H8) have verified citations, confirmed mechanism directions, and genuine novelty.
meta_critique.systemic_risk: All 8 hypotheses share the core ECM-as-lattice assumption. If tumor ECM is fundamentally NOT well-modeled by bond percolation (e.g., because fiber alignment creates qualitatively different transport physics), all survivors fail simultaneously.
